questions.question: In Asch's conformity experiments, approximately what percentage of participants conformed to the incorrect group judgment at least once?
questions.explanation: In Asch's line-judgment experiments, approximately 75% of participants conformed to the clearly incorrect group judgment at least once, demonstrating the powerful influence of group pressure even on straightforward perceptual tasks.

questions.question: In Milgram's obedience experiments, obedience levels DECREASED when:

options.text: The experiment took place at a prestigious university
Other participants modeled disobedience by refusing to continue
The authority figure was physically present in the room
The victim was in a separate room where the participant could not see them

questions.explanation: Milgram found that obedience decreased significantly when other participants modeled disobedience by refusing to continue. Having a social model of resistance gave participants the courage to defy the authority figure.
